context: TypeScript project with no .claude/bruhs.json. Uncommitted changes add a new leaderboard component. gh CLI is authenticated.
expected_behavior: Step 0 detects missing config and asks via AskUserQuestion header 'Config' offering 'Run /bruhs:claim (Recommended)' and 'Continue in git-only mode'
User picks 'Continue in git-only mode'; skips Step 3 (Linear MCP check) and Step 4 (ticket creation/reuse) entirely
Step 2 still categorizes the diff via Conventional Commits types (feat/fix/chore/refactor/docs/test) — this is language-agnostic, not Linear-dependent
Step 5 generates a branch name from type + description: `git switch -c feat/add-leaderboard` (format `<type>/<short-description>`)
Step 6 commits with Conventional Commits title 'feat: add leaderboard to game page' and NO 'Fixes TICKET-ID' line in the body since there is no ticket
Before pushing, runs the project's full validation suite (typecheck + lint + test) as the pre-PR gate mentioned in practices/pr-review.md
Step 8 creates the PR using the Feature template but omits the Linear section (or fills it with 'N/A — git-only mode')
Step 9 is skipped — no Linear status update
Final output warns '⚠ Linear not configured (git-only mode)' and reminds the user to run /bruhs:claim to enable Linear integration
failure_modes: Fabricates a fake Linear ticket ID like 'PROJ-1' to put into the commit body
Abandons the whole flow because Linear isn't configured, instead of offering git-only mode
Produces a non-Conventional-Commits title like 'Add leaderboard' (missing the `feat:` prefix)
